{{Infobox artifact
| name = Zenith Devices
| introduced = lh-01-the-captain
}}

''See also: [[Zenith Devices Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Zenith]] Devices are the original forms of [[Aethertech]], created by human technicians during a historical era known as the [[Zenith Era]]. These artifacts are characterized by immense power and advanced capabilities that far exceed modern technology, though they were designed for utility rather than comfort. While many consider them to be mere myths, several have been identified or awakened, including the starship known as [[The Last Horizon]], the [[Zenith Colony]] (also called [[Shadow Ark]]), a [[Zenith Cannon]], a [[Zenith Blade]], and a [[Zenith Processor]]. There are seven such devices in total. The functionality of these objects is highly adaptive, as their specific powers often depend on the individuals using them. However, they possess certain inherent traits; for example, the starship serves as a vessel, while the colony has the ability to hide and consume planets. Some devices are primarily intended for combat. Despite their incredible strength, the devices possess distinct personalities and can exhibit traits such as ego, competitiveness, or jealousy. They operate under specific rules, such as the starship's symbiotic relationship with a limited crew of six. Although they are naturally competitive with one another, their intended purpose was to foster the growth and flourishing of galactic civilization.}}

== The Captain ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Originally created by human [[Technicians]], [[Zenith]] Devices are characterized by a design philosophy that prioritizes utility over comfort. These machines, which include the [[Zenith]] Starship, are known to possess the capability to locate specific individuals. Every device within this category functions according to a set of highly specific rules and maintains a level of superiority over [[Aethertech]].}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Zenith Starship]] is one of the [[Zenith Devices]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c5">''The Captain'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Zenith Devices]] can find individuals.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c10">''The Captain'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Zenith Devices]] are designed with utility in mind rather than comfort.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c21">''The Captain'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|All [[Zenith Devices]] operate according to highly specific rules.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c28">''The Captain'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Zenith Devices]] are superior to [[Aethertech]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c33">''The Captain'', chapter 33</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The original [[Zenith Devices]] were created by human [[Technicians]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c35">''The Captain'', chapter 35</ref>}}

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Regarded as the most significant examples of [[Aethertech]], the seven [[Zenith]] Devices became the primary focus of a crew's search. These powerful entities were originally developed by various [[Aether]] [[Technicians]] and operate through a specialized crew system. Throughout their journey, it is revealed that these devices possess the capability to activate.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[Zenith Devices]] are considered the greatest [[Aethertech]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c18">''The Engineer'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The crew is searching for [[Zenith Devices]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c19">''The Engineer'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Some [[Aether]] [[Technicians]] worked on the [[Zenith Devices]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c20">''The Engineer'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Zenith Devices]] can activate.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c25">''The Engineer'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Zenith Devices]] are powerful entities that use a crew system.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c34">''The Engineer'', chapter 34</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|There are seven [[Zenith Devices]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c55">''The Engineer'', chapter 55</ref>}}

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Zenith Devices]] are described as priceless even when dormant.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c8">''The Knight'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Zenith Devices]] are fundamentally different from modern [[Aethertech]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c9">''The Knight'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Zenith Devices]] were examined by the narrator's father and his team.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c23">''The Knight'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|It is not the role of [[Zenith Devices]] to prevent people from acting like heroes.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c41">''The Knight'', chapter 41</ref>}}

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Originally created to ensure the prosperity of galactic civilization, the [[Zenith]] Devices first awakened as seven competitors whose immense power brought an end to the [[Zenith Era]]. These entities possess adaptive functions and include specialized models designed for both exploration and combat. Although [[Benri]] harbors deep distrust toward them based on past experiences, [[Solstice]] funded a massive hunt to locate the devices and currently possesses two of them. During an ambush on Shyrax, enemies utilized a pair of these devices to carry out their attack, though the [[Voice of Solstice]] maintains the ability to withhold certain secrets from them.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Benri]] expresses distrust toward [[Zenith Devices]] due to his past experiences.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c2">''The Pilot'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Solstice]] funded a massive hunt for the [[Zenith Devices]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c5">''The Pilot'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Zenith Devices]] have adaptive functions.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c12">''The Pilot'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Zenith Devices]] include entities designed for combat and exploration.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c15">''The Pilot'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|A pair of [[Zenith Devices]] were used by enemies to ambush Shyrax.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c21">''The Pilot'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Zenith Devices]] originally awakened as seven competitors whose might ended the [[Zenith Era]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c23">''The Pilot'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Zenith Devices]] were made to make galactic civilization flourish.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c24">''The Pilot'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Solstice]] possesses two [[Zenith Devices]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c34">''The Pilot'', chapter 34</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Voice of Solstice]] does not need to reveal all secrets to the [[Zenith Devices]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c39">''The Pilot'', chapter 39</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|“—required to sync with Aethertech, which I believe to be the fundamental difference between modern Aethertech and the Zenith Devices.”<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c9" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|“You know why the Zenith Devices were made, don’t you? We want galactic civilization to flourish.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c24" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|“I’ll show you. But I don’t need to give all my secrets to the Zenith Devices.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c39" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{The Last Horizon nav}}

[[Category:Artifacts]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon artifacts]]
